content.problemStatement:
You have NNN toffees and 333 friends. You will divide the toffees among your friends (You are too generous to take one yourself).

You want to make sure that your division is fair. Hence, you want to minimize the imbalance of your division.

Here, the imbalance is defined as (mx−mn)(\text{mx} - \text{mn})(mx−mn), where mx\text{mx}mx is the maximum toffees any friend received, and mn\text{mn}mn is the minimum toffees any friend received.

For example, if the you give 3,1,43, 1, 43,1,4 toffees to your friends, then mx=4\text{mx} = 4mx=4 and mn=1\text{mn} = 1mn=1, so the imbalance is 4−1=34-1 = 34−1=3.

Find the **minimum possible** imbalance.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single integer NNN - the number of toffees you had.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible imbalance.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
You can give each of your friends 111 toffee, and this has an imbalance of 000 since all get equal toffees.

Explanation:
You can give friend 111 222 toffees and friends 222 and 333, 333 toffees each. This makes mx=3\text{mx} = 3mx=3 and mn=2\text{mn} = 2mn=2, hence the imbalance is 111.
